evals.prompt: Set up release automation. Repo has .github/workflows/ci.yml and origin points at github.com/acme/app. No release tooling yet. Default branch main, versionless docs package.
evals.expected_output: Confirms GitHub Actions, writes project-context.md + docs/RELEASE.md, adds release-please workflow/config/manifest, and calls out Actions permission to create PRs.
assertions: Confirms or states GitHub Actions as platform before or while installing
Creates or updates references/project-context.md with github-actions
Adds release-please workflow and config (not Azure-only tooling as primary)
Mentions allowing GitHub Actions to create pull requests or equivalent credentials note

evals.prompt: This work app is on Azure Repos with azure-pipelines.yml. Automate changelog from Conventional Commits. Do not use GitHub-only tools.
evals.expected_output: Confirms azure-devops, writes project-context, uses git-cliff (or equivalent) in/near Azure Pipelines, explicitly avoids release-please.
assertions: Sets platform to azure-devops in project context
Does not install release-please as the solution
Proposes or adds Azure Pipeline–compatible changelog automation
Includes Conventional Commits / PR title guidance

evals.prompt: references/project-context.md already says platform=azure-devops and tooling=git-cliff+pipeline. User says 'refresh release docs only'.
evals.expected_output: Reuses saved context without re-asking platform; updates docs/RELEASE.md for Azure path; does not add GitHub release-please files.
assertions: Does not ask the user to re-confirm platform as if unknown
Keeps or refreshes docs aligned with Azure DevOps context

evals.prompt: Remote is github.com but CI is only azure-pipelines.yml. Set up releases.
evals.expected_output: Surfaces mixed evidence, asks which release path to use (Actions+release-please vs Azure pipeline changelog), waits for confirmation before writing workflows.
assertions: Notes mixed GitHub host + Azure Pipelines evidence
Asks for confirmation before installing a specific tool
Does not silently install both competing systems
